summary: Sanofi CEO Brandicourt contacted Medivation CEO Hung on an unsolicited basis and requested a telephone discussion.
Brandicourt and Hung had an initial telephone discussion during which Brandicourt expressed interest in pursuing a possible strategic transaction but did not indicate a potential price.

subject_names: Four industry participants who expressed interest in late March 2016
summary: Following media reports of Sanofi's acquisition interest, three industry participants contacted J. P. Morgan and a fourth contacted Dr. Hung to express interest in participating in any strategic discussions.
Sanofi submitted an unsolicited non-binding proposal to acquire Medivation for $52.50 per share, subject to due diligence, definitive agreements, and Sanofi board approval.

summary: Pfizer's Giordano contacted Medivation CEO Hung to indicate an interest in participating in any strategic discussions Medivation might initiate.
Medivation board determined to engage Evercore as an additional independent financial advisor.
Sanofi issued a press release publicly announcing its $52.50 proposal for Medivation.
Medivation board unanimously rejected Sanofi's $52.50 proposal, concluding it substantially undervalued Medivation.
Sanofi issued a press release restating the terms of its $52.50 proposal and threatening to commence a consent solicitation to remove and replace the Medivation board.
Sanofi filed a preliminary consent solicitation statement with the SEC seeking removal and replacement of the Medivation board.
Medivation board directed J. P. Morgan and Evercore to contact select industry participants to determine levels of preliminary interest in a strategic process.
J. P. Morgan and Evercore contacted eleven industry participants, and Medivation contacted a twelfth, to explore preliminary interest in a potential strategic transaction.
